design_rationale: After stripping 9,909w of Scherer 1933 dissertation apparatus from Section 1, the letter yields 11,728w — exactly 3 balanced episodes (~3,900w each). EP01 covers the opening frame through the soul's journey (S01-S11). EP02 covers the daily disciplines, the Sufi way, and Hatim al-Asamm (S12-S18). EP03 opens with the guiding shaykh and closes with the eight admonitions (S19-S24). All episodes exceed the 2,800w minimum floor. Segmentation performed agent-inline (no Anthropic API key required).

episodes.title: The Frame and the Problem of Knowledge
episodes.theme: The occasion of the letter — why knowledge without action is the soul's greatest trap
episodes.opening_tension: A student who has mastered every science asks: which of this knowledge will help me in the grave?
episodes.closing_resolution: The listener understands that knowledge divorced from action is a burden, not a gift, and that the soul's journey begins with this recognition.

episodes.title: The Disciplines of the Path
episodes.theme: The daily and nightly disciplines that transform abstract knowledge into lived submission
episodes.opening_tension: Knowing the problem is not enough — what does the path actually require of the seeker day after day?
episodes.closing_resolution: Hatim al-Asamm's eight benefits crystallise the whole argument: knowledge becomes benefit only through practice, patience, and surrender.

episodes.title: The Guiding Shaykh and the Final Counsels
episodes.theme: The role of the living guide and the eight closing admonitions that seal Ghazali's counsel
episodes.opening_tension: Can a seeker travel the path alone, or is a living guide indispensable?
episodes.closing_resolution: Ghazali closes with eight precise admonitions — four to abandon, four to practice — sealing the letter with the counsel that cannot be written but must be lived.
